case_number.description: Case number is the primary docket identifier for linking filings across a matter; must be extracted exactly as formatted to ensure accurate docket tracking and e-filing compliance

filing_date.description: Filing date is the legal anchor for calculating deadlines, statutes of limitations, and response windows; even a one-day error can constitute a missed deadline with procedural consequences

court_name.description: Court name determines jurisdiction, applicable procedural rules, and routing to the responsible legal team; errors cause misrouting of matter management tasks

bar_number.description: Attorney bar number is required for e-filing system validation and professional responsibility tracking; inaccurate values prevent filing acceptance

filing_type.description: Filing type determines routing to litigation workflows (discovery, motions calendar, hearing scheduling) and sets response deadline calculation logic

case_name.description: Case name is used for display and search in matter management systems; must match the official case caption

plaintiff_name.description: Plaintiff identity is required for matter management, conflict-of-interest checks, and case strategy routing

defendant_name.description: Defendant identity is required for conflict-of-interest checks, OFAC screening, and litigation risk assessment

hearing_date.description: Hearing date drives calendar management and deadline reminders for litigation teams; errors can cause missed court appearances

attorney_name.description: Attorney name is required for matter management and professional responsibility tracking

jury_demand.description: Jury demand status affects trial scheduling and litigation strategy; false negatives may waive jury rights under applicable rules
